title: Sort an Array
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C++ proposals. The minimum-memory champion (in-place heapsort) is implemented in C; see crossReferences.

variants.languageReason: std::sort is introsort with an inlined comparator: fast on any input, O(log n) stack, no range assumption. Best general balance.
A tight native counting-sort loop is comparison-free and linear; the bounded value range (R = 100001) makes it the raw-speed winner.
variants.approachJustification: Counting sort over the offset range.

reasoningSummary: Comparison sorts are O(n log n); the small fixed value range lets counting sort beat that on raw speed at O(R) memory, while in-place heapsort (C) reaches O(1) extra memory. Introsort is the balanced default.
timeComplexity: O(n log n) recommended / O(n + R) (counting)
